page_title: Express Entry: Language test results - Canada.ca

h2: How long your results are valid for

sections.heading: On this page
bullets: Take a language test

How long your results are valid for
Check your language level

sections.heading: Take a language test
items: You must prove your language skills by taking an approved language test.
To do this, you must:
bullets: schedule your test with an agency approved by IRCC and pay the costs
enter the test results into your Express Entry profile (and the language test result form or certificate number and test PIN, if the system asks for it)

sections.heading: Language tests we accept
items: You can take any of these approved language tests:

sections.heading: Language requirements
items: To measure your English or French levels, we use:
bullets: Canadian Language Benchmarks (CLB) for English
Niveaux de compétence linguistique canadien (NCLC) for French

sections.heading: How long your results are valid for
items: Your test results must be less than 2 years old when you:
bullets: complete your Express Entry profile, and
submit your application for permanent residence

sections.heading: Check your language level
items: You can use your test results to find your CLB level.

sections.heading: Federal Skilled Worker Program
items: First official language (maximum 24 points)
Second official language (4 points if you meet the minimum threshold in each of the 4 language abilities)

sections.heading: How to calculate your points
bullets: First official language: Speaking + Listening + Reading + Writing = your subtotal
Speaking + Listening + Reading + Writing = your subtotal
Second official language: Add 4 points to your subtotal if you meet the minimum level in all 4 abilities. If you do not, you get 0 points for your second official language.
Add 4 points to your subtotal if you meet the minimum level in all 4 abilities. If you do not, you get 0 points for your second official language.
Add (1) and (2) together for your total points.

Add (1) and (2) together for your total points.

sections.heading: Federal Skilled Worker Program
items: First official language (maximum 24 points)
Second official language (4 points if you meet the minimum threshold in each of the 4 language abilities)

sections.heading: Federal Skilled Worker Program
items: Your language level will be based on the “ Équivalence ancien score ” (previous score equivalency).
First official language (maximum 24 points)
Second official language (4 points if you meet the minimum threshold in each of the 4 language abilities)
The testing organization will issue you a copy of TEF Canada electronic test results. The results will include:
bullets: Only enter the scores for each language ability from that column.
If you don’t enter the correct scores, we may refuse your application.

Your attestation de résultats (statement of results) will also include test results in the column marked “Score / 699.” Don’t enter these results in your Express Entry profile. They aren’t compatible with our system at this time.
your score
the NCLC equivalence level you achieved for each test component
